title: persona-config
description: Copied to .claude/persona-config.json by setup-personas. The hook scripts read this at runtime via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}-relative scripts + a project-relative config path, so the plugin's hook scripts stay generic across every project.

testAndLintCommand.description: Single shell command the stop-gate runs; non-zero exit = BLOCK. e.g. 'npm test && npm run lint'

lintCommand.description: Formatter/linter invoked on a single changed file, e.g. 'npx eslint --fix'. The file path is appended as the last argument.

graphUpdateCommand.description: Code Review Graph's incremental-update command, taking a file path argument, e.g. 'npx code-review-graph update'

sourceGlobs.description: Shell glob patterns, PROJECT-ROOT-RELATIVE (not absolute), identifying source files worth graph-indexing. The hook normalizes the tool's absolute file_path against CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR before matching. If empty, every edited file is passed to graphUpdateCommand.

protectedPaths.description: Shell glob patterns, PROJECT-ROOT-RELATIVE (not absolute - e.g. 'supabase/migrations/*', not '/home/u/proj/supabase/migrations/*'), for paths requiring explicit human approval before Write/Edit. Examples: 'supabase/migrations/*', '*.lock', 'generated/*'

gatedAgents.description: Agent-type names the stop-gate's test+lint check applies to on SubagentStop. Defaults to just lead-programmer so cheap/high-frequency personas (explorer, repo-historian) never pay the check's cost. Add a persona name here if you add a new code-writing persona - this is a config edit, not a plugin file edit.

pluginVersion.description: The seb-personas plugin version this project was last ADAPTed/updated at, stamped by setup-personas. Compared against the installed plugin's current version by the SessionStart drift-check hook.

personaSelection.description: Which optional personas (planner, repo-historian, reviewer, researcher, milestone-auditor) this project chose to include, recorded by setup-personas' persona-selection wizard so --update mode knows which files to re-derive. orchestrator/explorer/lead-programmer are always included and not listed here.

issueTracker.description: Human-readable note on the tracker chosen during ADAPT, e.g. 'GitHub issues labeled plan/<slug>; fetch with gh issue list --label plan/<slug>' or 'local files in docs/plans/<slug>/issues/'
